raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX I received a notice from XXXX  that my XXXX score and rating had decreased. I was shocked at the decline and opened a dispute saying i didn't remember owning an XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  Card CBNA opened in XX/XX/XXXX ( almost XXXX  years before ). 
Information came back that the account was 'open ' and charged {$81.00}. In XX/XX/XXXX, the account was XXXX past due, then as of XX/XX/XXXX the payment was XXXX overdue and the balance was {$160.00}. 
I called Citi Cards with a number provided by XXXX. I said I did not have that card and did not make any purchases. The individual I spoke to ( XXXX XXXX said Citi would investigate the complaint as fraud, because I had no knowledge or use of the card. He closed the account as of XXXX PM on XX/XX/XXXX. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a resolution notice saying the " company that reported the information has certified to XXXX  that the information is accurate. This item was not changed as a result of our processing of your dispute. '' An XXXX  credit report was attached showing my exemplary record of debt payment ( with the exception of this disputed new charge of {$160.00}. ) I called XXXX back to ask what to do. XXXX  then allowed my personal credit report to be blocked. Another dispute was opened at XXXX PM. To comply with the requirements this XXXX dispute engendered, I must file a report with a government entity. I am completing this report to satisfy that requirement. 
I am so frustrated. Some crook got hold of ancient credit card information and has seriously injured my credit. How do I turn this situation around? It's fraud -- but I am the one anxiously trying to restore my credit. WHAT CAN I DO TO FIX THIS?
